structure.notes: The note has the required Stage 0 sections, labelled assumptions, a PartialID-appropriate §7 bounding functional, and an actionable §14 checklist. The locked tuple at lines 134-151 matches the PartialID anchor and does not drift.

novelty.notes: The actually derived kernel is below novelty_target=field: the LP sharpness result is mostly the standard finite-polytope projection argument, and the sign result is an existential two-bin witness whose key margins and perturbation persistence are assumed rather than derived. Assessed tier would be subfield/incremental unless strengthened into a genuinely new sharp/tighter bound or regime-opening theorem.

correctness.notes: The main sign-certificate proof has a circular assumption and several proof gaps; the finite LP proof also mis-scales conditional copula constraints for nondegenerate X and leaves sensitivity linearization underspecified.

perItemFindings.label: Assumption witness / Proposition sign-certificate
perItemFindings.one_line: Assumption 8 already assumes the small-(Gamma,eta) persistence that Proposition 2 claims to prove, making that part circular.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition sign-certificate
perItemFindings.one_line: The claimed critical radius rho* and feasibility of the lower-quarter pairing at large rho are asserted via continuity/monotonicity without an explicit feasible construction or exact threshold calculation.

perItemFindings.label: Lemma polytope
perItemFindings.one_line: The conditional copula-tube inequalities are multiplied by the X-stratum mass but keep right-hand side rho rather than rho Q(X=x), so the displayed finite constraints are not equivalent for nondegenerate X.

perItemFindings.label: Sensitivity LP formulation
perItemFindings.one_line: The claim that the continuous-treatment marginal sensitivity restriction is linear after adding tilt variables is underspecified; the note does not spell out the variables and normalizations enough to verify no bilinear terms remain.

perItemFindings.label: Novelty tier
perItemFindings.one_line: The derivation proves a standard finite LP projection plus a simple copula-tail witness under saturated marginal assumptions; this is below the enforced field-tier floor.

verbatim_critique:
Structure is adequate. The note preserves the PartialID locked tuple in lines 134-151, keeps the skeleton sections, labels the assumptions used by the proofs, and the §14 checklist at lines 499-535 separates probabilistic objects from deterministic identities and names the proof dependencies. I would not block Stage 1 on structure alone.

The main problem is novelty and correctness of the flagship theorem. Proposition \ref{prop:lp-sharpness} at lines 262-268 is mostly a standard finite-dimensional partial-identification fact: once the feasible set is a nonempty compact convex polytope and the target has a fixed positive denominator, the image is a compact interval and endpoints are LP optima. That is useful scaffolding but not field-tier mathematics. Proposition \ref{prop:sign-certificate} is the intended substantive result, but in its current form it is only an existential two-bin example with the adjacent-dose quantile functions fixed by a saturated moment vector in Assumption \ref{ass:witness} at lines 198-203. This makes the IV/sensitivity part largely assumed rather than derived from observable restrictions. Under the orchestrator floor, the current derivation would be at most subfield/incremental, not field.

There is also a circularity in the sign-certificate theorem. Assumption \ref{ass:witness}, lines 198-203, states that for Gamma>1 sufficiently close to one and eta>0 sufficiently small, the same strict inequalities persist by finite LP continuity. Proposition \ref{prop:sign-certificate}, lines 272-278, then claims exactly the existence of such gamma/eta/rho neighborhoods, and the proof repeats the same LP-continuity assertion at lines 454-455. The reference calculation at Gamma=1, eta=0 is valid as a simple copula-tail lower bound, but the perturbation part is not proved independently because it is built into the assumption.

The finite-polytope proof needs repair. In Lemma \ref{lem:polytope}, the conditional copula restriction is stated as |C_{01,x}(u,v)-min(u,v)| <= rho in lines 185-190. After multiplying by Q(X=x), the finite inequality should have right-hand side rho Q(X=x), not rho as displayed at lines 337-340. The displayed constraint is not equivalent except in a unit-mass stratum, and it weakens the tube in small strata. The same lemma also says the marginal sensitivity restriction is linear after introducing tilt variables, lines 335-336, but the note never defines the finite tilt variables, their normalization, or their relation to the cell probabilities precisely enough to verify linearity rather than hidden bilinearity.

The critical-radius claim in Proposition \ref{prop:sign-certificate} is underproved. Lines 446-452 establish monotonicity in rho and appeal to continuity, then assert that at sufficiently large rho the lower-quarter countermonotone pairing is feasible and negative. The counterexample at lines 487-494 gives the unrestricted pairing, but the proof does not compute its copula distance from comonotonicity on tau x [0,1] or show the value is feasible for a stated rho<1. To claim a critical radius rho* where the lower endpoint crosses zero, the note should either construct the feasible law and bound its tube radius explicitly, or solve the finite witness LP and display the threshold.

Proposition \ref{prop:no-tube} is acceptable as a minimality direction only if the intended statement is containment. However the surrounding prose at lines 304 and 483 says the bound 'collapses' or 'recovers' the product of one-dose envelopes, while the theorem at lines 293-301 proves only subset containment under a pasting condition. The text should either strengthen the theorem to equality with all required assumptions or weaken the prose to match the theorem.

To reach an ACCEPT under novelty_target=field, the revision needs more than prose. A plausible repair path is to make the witness fully explicit as a finite observable law and finite constraint matrix, remove the perturbation conclusion from Assumption \ref{ass:witness}, prove the LP-continuity neighborhood from stated compactness/nondegeneracy hypotheses, and either derive an exact or sharply bounded rho threshold for the sign certificate. To satisfy the field-tier floor, the main theorem should also be framed as a genuinely new sharp/tighter bound or regime-opening copula-tube result, not just the standard LP representation plus a saturated two-bin illustration.
